perturbation.original_text: Under this Agreement, i-on will provide the following limited services for the Hosted Site: 1. connectivity to the Internet via a T1 (that may be shared by other Web sites) to a leading Internet backbone access provider such as UUNET, and reasonable efforts to maintain such connectivity with the phone company and the Internet backbone access provider;
perturbation.changed_text: Under this Agreement, i-on will provide the following limited services for the Hosted Site: 1. connectivity to the Internet.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific details of the connectivity (T1 line, shared access, UUNET backbone), the clause becomes vague. While i-on still promises 'connectivity', the quality and nature of that connectivity are no longer clearly defined. This introduces uncertainty and contradicts the subsequent clause that it will provide reasonable efforts to maintain such connectivity with the phone company and the Internet backbone access provider, because there is not backbone specified and this statement may be seen as not applicable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Under this Agreement, i-on will provide the following limited services for the Hosted Site: 1. connectivity to the Internet. connectivity with the phone company and the Internet backbone access provider; 2. use of the Hosting Computer (that may be shared by other Web sites) as described in this Agreement and maintenance required to keep such Hosting Computer in good working order; 3. physical space for the Hosting Computer at a facility that maintains proper environmental conditions in the area(s) where the Hosting Computer is located and maintains reasonable efforts to prevent unauthorized access to the physical location of the Hosting Computer; 4. an emergency electrical power backup system for the Hosting Computer; 5. up to 150 MB of mirrored computer storage on the Hosting Computer; 6. archival backups of such mirrored computer storage on a weekly basis;
perturbation.justification: The original text specified the type of internet connectivity, including a T1 line and UUNET backbone. The changed text removes these details, leaving only 'connectivity to the Internet.' The contradicted text, which is essentially a restatement of the changed text, continues to mention 'connectivity with the phone company and the Internet backbone access provider'. By omitting the specific details of the 'Internet backbone access provider' in the changed text, the subsequent reference to maintaining connectivity with the 'Internet backbone access provider' in the contradicted text creates a contradiction, because the specific backbone provider is no longer defined, potentially making that part of the agreement unenforceable or at least ambiguous. This aligns with the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 13. up to 1 hour per month of Web site administration services at no additional charge, limited to: requests for changes to ftp/e-mail users and passwords; requests for e-mail configuration changes; modification of mail aliases; changes to server MIME types; files restored from backup; answering questions about server-side scripts; ftp configuration changes; log file configuration changes; importing or exporting of database records; and consultation on site operation and administration. Additional Web site administration services will be billed at $200 per hour.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. Web site administration services. Additional Web site administration services will be billed at $200 per hour.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the 'up to 1 hour per month of Web site administration services at no additional charge' creates an omission. Now, all Web site administration services will be billed at $200 per hour. This omission contradicts the implication that some level of service was included, as it now states that additional Web site administration services will be billed, thus creating an in-text contradiction that there was something free before to begin with.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. up to 10 mailboxes accessible via the POP3 mail protocol that are mapped to the Hosted Site's Internet address; 13. Web site administration services. Additional Web site administration services will be billed at $200 per hour. 14. a monthly report of user activity on the Hosted Site. RESPONSIBILITIES OF THE CUSTOMER The Customer is responsible for paying i-on the recurring monthly fee in the amount of $450.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the customer was entitled to 'up to 1 hour per month of Web site administration services at no additional charge'. The changed text removes this entitlement, implying all web administration services will be billed at $200 per hour. This omission directly contradicts the prior expectation within the document and the contradicted text that some services were included free of charge. The contradicted text refers to Web site administration services directly after the number of mailboxes accessible which provides implicit continuation of the same included benefits, triggering a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY i-on will not be liable under any circumstances for any lost profits or other consequential damages, even if i-on has been advised as to the possibility of such damages. i-on's liability for damages to the Customer for any cause whatsoever, regardless of the form of action, and whether in contract or in tort, including negligence, shall be limited to one (1) month's fees and the remaining portion of any prepaid fees.
perturbation.changed_text: LIMITATION OF LIABILITY i-on will not be liable under any circumstances for any lost profits or other consequential damages, even if i-on has been advised as to the possibility of such damages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'i-on's liability for damages to the Customer for any cause whatsoever, regardless of the form of action, and whether in contract or in tort, including negligence, shall be limited to one (1) month's fees and the remaining portion of any prepaid fees', the entire limitation of liability clause is now incomplete. While it still disclaims liability for lost profits, it provides no cap on liability for other types of damages. This creates uncertainty and could contradict with other parts of the contract, if some service promises can be infered, now liability is unlimited
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION The Customer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless i-on, against any lawsuits, claims, damages, or liabilities (or actions or proceedings in respect thereof) to which i-on may become subject related to or arising out of Customer's use of i-on's services, and will reimburse i-on for all legal and other expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred in connection with investigating, defending, or settling any such loss, claim, damage, liability, action, or proceeding whether or not in connection with pending or threatened litigation in which i-on is a party. The provisions of this Agreement relating to indemnification shall survive termination of the Customer's Hosted Site.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited i-on's liability for damages to one month's fees. The changed text removes this limitation. The indemnification clause requires the customer to indemnify i-on against any damages. Because the liability limit has been removed in the changed text, this now creates a contradiction. i-on's liability is now effectively unlimited under the contract in the modified excerpt. The customer would have unlimited liability to cover i-on in all cases from the 'INDEMNIFICATION' clause, even though the original text had capped i-on's potential liability at one months fees. This is a strong contradiction that creates uncertainty and would cause potential legal issues.
